title: cuphea trochilus
intro: Cuphea trochilus, also known as the hummingbird flower, is a perennial plant native to Mexico and the southern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Drinking Cuphea trochilus tea may also help to boost the immune system and provide relief from coughs and congestion.
